name: Credit Bank of Moscow (public joint-stock company)
summary: Credit Bank of Moscow public joint-stock company provides various banking products and services in Russia. The company operates through six segments: Corporate Business, Retail Banking, Investment, Treasury, Cash Operations, and Subsidiary Banks. The Corporate Business segment offers corporate and overdraft lending, factoring, financial and operating leasing, letters of credit, guarantees, corporate deposit, and settlements and money transfer services. The Retail Banking segment provides retail demand and term deposit services; retail lending, including cash loans, car loans and mortgages, and private banking services; and banking card products, as well as settlements services. The Investment segment engages in the provision of securities trading and brokerage in securities, repo transactions, and foreign exchange services. The Treasury segment offers interbank lending and borrowings from banks, as well as issues domestic bonds and promissory notes. The Cash Operations segment engages in cash handling, calculation, and transportation activities. The Subsidiary Banks segment consists of JSC √Ç¬ìRUSNARBANK√Ç¬î, Investment Bank VESTA (LLC), and LLC √Ç¬ìBank SKS√Ç¬î. It operates through 132 branches, 1,008 ATMs, and 6,784 payment terminals. The company was founded in 1992 and is based in Moscow, Russia. Credit Bank of Moscow public joint-stock company is a subsidiary of Limited Liability Company Concern ROSSIUM
